name: loop_slice_gap
description: Loop Slice 'Gap' option (task 0253; direction confirmed + cap topology corrected by the LIVE reference capture in task 0261). Reuses the 0251/0252 unit cube (8 verts / 12 edges / 6 quad faces); the seed edge is the vertical edge (1,-1,-1)-(1,1,-1), so the ring cuts a horizontal belt around the 4 side faces at y=0 (4 vertical rails). Split (always ON here) duplicates each rail midpoint into a coincident lo/hi pair; Cap Sections (ON) seals each shell's boundary loop with ONE cap polygon (16 verts / 24 edges / 12 faces, two independent closed shells). Gap opens a width between the two shells by pushing each seam pair apart ALONG ITS RAIL (cut) direction, symmetric about the split line — exactly what the reference does: gap G moves each vert G/2 toward the corner on ITS OWN side of the loop. With G=0.4 the 4 rails are vertical (±y), so one shell's loop moves to y=+0.2 and the other to y=-0.2 (symmetric, so the vertex SET is the same either way). The 8 loop verts, coincident at 4 midpoints while G=0, now occupy 8 distinct positions {[±1,±0.2,±1]} — proof the two shells separated by exactly G, opening a REAL visible band between the two horizontal caps. Topology is UNCHANGED from the caps-on case (16 verts / 24 edges / 12 faces); Gap only relocates the duplicated verts. The gap_zero case pins the byte-for-byte invariant: G=0 leaves every seam pair coincident (the two caps coincide), identical to 0251/0252. Reference-captured geometry law: each seam pair separates by exactly G along its pre-op rail direction; nothing else moves.
source: LIVE-captured from the reference app (task 0261) — driven headlessly through the reference app's command port (no mouse): build cube → select seed edge → activate Loop Slice → set count/position/split/caps/gap via tool attributes → apply → drop → dump verts+faces. Full drive transcript + raw dumps in the private toolcard (toolcards/mesh.loopSlice/findings.md + capture/). The real geometry: gap pushes the two split shells apart ALONG THE RAIL (±Y on the cube, the loop's perpendicular rail direction), symmetric about the cut — on the reference's ±0.5 cube a gap of 0.2 world put one shell's loop at y=+0.1 and the other at y=-0.1 (each moved gap/2). vibe3d's rail-direction gap was already correct; the DIVERGENCE fixed in 0261 was the CAP topology (single-polygon-per-shell, leaving the band open — see loop_slice_caps.json), which is why the gap is now a real visible band instead of an invisible in-plane fill. Coordinates here are the reference capture scaled from the ±0.5 reference cube to this ±1 fixture cube (gap 0.4 → y=±0.2); the direction + symmetry + topology are captured verbatim.

provenance.notes: Driven headlessly via command port; coordinates scaled from the reference's +/-0.5 cube to this +/-1 fixture cube (gap 0.4 -> y=+/-0.2).
